---
lang: en-GB
output: 
  pdf_document:
    latex_engine: xelatex
    includes:
      in_header: Math.Rnw
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, comment = NA)
library(dplyr)

# ---- CONFIGURATION ----
num_problems <- 20    # Total problems to generate
max_digit <- 99       # Max number for operations
min_digit <- 10       # Min number for operations
type_problems <- "multiply" # Options: "add", "subtract", "multiply", "divide", "mix"
# -----------------------

# Function to generate math structures
generate_problems <- function(type, n) {
  set.seed(sample(1:10000, 1)) 
  
  if(type == "addition") {
    num1 <- sample(min_digit:max_digit, n, replace = TRUE)
    num2 <- sample(min_digit:max_digit, n, replace = TRUE)
    op <- "+"
    ans <- num1 + num2
  } else if(type == "subtraction") {
    num1 <- sample(min_digit:max_digit, n, replace = TRUE)
    num2 <- sample(min_digit:max_digit, n, replace = TRUE)
    actual_num1 <- pmax(num1, num2)
    actual_num2 <- pmin(num1, num2)
    num1 <- actual_num1
    num2 <- actual_num2
    op <- "-"
    ans <- num1 - num2
  } else if(type == "multiplication") {
    num1 <- sample(2:12, n, replace = TRUE) 
    num2 <- sample(2:12, n, replace = TRUE)
    op <- "\\times"
    ans <- num1 * num2
  } else if(type == "division") {
    num2 <- sample(2:10, n, replace = TRUE) 
    ans <- sample(2:10, n, replace = TRUE)  
    num1 <- num2 * ans                      
    op <- "\\div"
  }
  
  return(data.frame(num1, num2, op, ans, type = type))
}
```

\begin{flushright}
\textbf{Name:} \underline{\hspace{5cm}} \\[0.3cm]
\textbf{Date:} \underline{\hspace{5cm}}
\end{flushright}

\vspace{0.5cm}

\begin{multicols}{3}
```{r, results='asis'}
# 1. Determine and generate the problem pool based on your parameter
if (type_problems == "add") {
  all_problems <- generate_problems("addition", num_problems)
} else if (type_problems == "subtract") {
  all_problems <- generate_problems("subtraction", num_problems)
} else if (type_problems == "multiply") {
  all_problems <- generate_problems("multiplication", num_problems)
} else if (type_problems == "divide") {
  all_problems <- generate_problems("division", num_problems)
} else if (type_problems == "mix") {
  # Mixes addition and subtraction evenly (adjust as needed)
  add_probs <- generate_problems("addition", ceiling(num_problems / 2))
  sub_probs <- generate_problems("subtraction", floor(num_problems / 2))
  all_problems <- rbind(add_probs, sub_probs) %>% sample_frac(1) 
}

# 2. Print loop with layout detection
for(i in 1:nrow(all_problems)) {
  
  # For division, horizontal layout look better (e.g., 20 ÷ 5 = ___)
  if (all_problems$type[i] == "division") {
    cat(paste0(i, ") \\quad $", all_problems$num1[i], " ", all_problems$op[i], " ", all_problems$num2[i], " = \\quad $ \\vspace{1.5cm}\n\n"))
  } else {
    # Vertical stacking for addition, subtraction, and multiplication
    cat(paste0(i, ") \\quad $", "\\begin{array}{r}", all_problems$num1[i], "\\\\ ", all_problems$op[i], " ", all_problems$num2[i], "\\\\ \\hline \\end{array}$", "\\vspace{1.5cm}\n\n"))
  }
  
}
```
\end{multicols}
